title: paracarphalea kirondron subsp. geayi
intro: Paracarphalea kirondron subsp. geayi, also known as the Geayi Carphalea, is a lesser-known plant species within the family Rubiaceae. It is a type of shrub or small tree native to tropical regions of Madagascar. The Paracarphalea kirondron subsp. geayi has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever and rheumatism, due to the presence of bioactive compounds like flavonoids and alkaloids. Its herbal preparations, often in the form of infusions or decoctions, are believed to provide relief from pain and inflammation, as well as other health benefits such as antioxidant and antimicrobial activities.
